claim: Contaminated unlabeled logs hurt naive BC
evidence: PointNav all-data BC drops from 0.460 to 0.153 as bad fraction rises from 50% to 95%; Lift MG all-demo reaches 0.200 at 20k and 31/150 pooled on three fresh frozen splits; Can MG all-demo reaches 0.100 at 20k; frozen Can 40p/80b all-demo is the weakest pooled endpoint row at 81/150 versus 99/150 TRIAGE-BC, 90/150 weighted BC, and 108/150 positive-only NN
caveat: Requires strong backbone for robotics interpretation; frozen Can split 22 is an exception where all-demo exceeds TRIAGE-BC

claim: Scarce positive and bad labels can recover useful support
evidence: PointNav score-gap demo BC gets 1.000 success across 50/75/90/95% bad; the equal label-budget-5 and label-budget-2 PointNav sweeps also get 1.000 score-gap success across all four bad fractions; with 5 positives, the bad-label-count sweep shows 1/2/5 bad demos are enough for near-perfect or perfect score-gap demo BC and pure selected support; development Can balanced support has 0.962 aggregate purity and 0.900 success at 20k; Square and Transport relative-quality labels recover high-purity support on new task families
caveat: Robotics rows validate support selection plus BC-RNN-GMM
source: not full inverse-Q; fresh balanced Can audits favor positive-only NN top80 over bad-aware support; Square/Transport are support-side relative-quality diagnostics

claim: Purity and coverage must be adapted
evidence: Development balanced Can favors coverage-gap; development 20p/80b favored top20 precision; the frozen Can 40p/80b support sweep shows classifier top-k moving from high precision/low recall to high recall/high contamination, while TRIAGE recovers 110/120 hidden positives but admits 80/240 hidden bad demos; frozen Lift classifier-score top160 has high support purity but only 68/150 pooled endpoint success; fresh Can 20p/80b support audit has TRIAGE recover 54/60 hidden positives but admit 69/240 hidden bad demos, while positive-only NN top20 recovers 49/60 and admits 11/240; fresh Can 80p/80b split 33 has TRIAGE purity 0.975 but lower endpoint success than positive-only NN
caveat: Needs an explicit algorithmic converter in the final method; high-purity fixed top-k is not enough on Lift
source: and fresh Can diagnostics increasingly favor positive-only NN unless bad-aware conversion improves coverage and action quality

claim: Hard support can beat weighted BC at fixed later checkpoints
evidence: Can 40p/80b masscap reaches 0.733 versus weighted 0.567 at 20k, and remains ahead in a three-seed 50-episode endpoint check: 0.680 versus 0.573; fresh Can 20p/80b split 11 gives TRIAGE-BC 33/50 versus weighted BC 18/50; Lift pos-min reaches 0.667 versus weighted 0.533 at the original 20k endpoint and 0.600 versus 0.533 over 50 episodes; fresh frozen Lift rows now favor weighted BC, 93/150 versus 74/150 for TRIAGE-BC
caveat: Weighted BC is competitive and sometimes wins decisively; positive-only NN beats TRIAGE-BC on frozen Can 40p/80b

claim: Primary frozen final Can 40p/80b splits favor TRIAGE-BC over weighted BC but not positive-only NN
evidence: Under METHOD_FREEZE.md, split seeds 11, 22, and 33 all favor TRIAGE-BC over weighted BC at the fixed 20k endpoint: pooled success is 99/150 for TRIAGE-BC versus 90/150 for weighted BC. Positive-only NN top40 reaches 108/150 and beats TRIAGE on two of three splits. All-demo BC reaches 81/150 pooled, below TRIAGE, weighted BC, and positive-only NN. Diagnostic all-positive oracle reaches 147/150
caveat: This supports hard selected support over soft weighted sampling and pooled mixed-log cloning
source: but not a bad-label benefit claim on Can 40p/80b; all-demo exceeds TRIAGE on split 22; oracle is diagnostic only and shows large remaining support-conversion headroom

claim: Soft weighted BC can be better when broad coverage matters
evidence: Can MG weighted sampler beats matched all-positive, all-demo, and pos-p10 controls at fixed 20k: 0.333 versus 0.200, 0.100, and 0.167. The staged branch-proxy check shows simple likelihood proxies choose all-positive support on original Can MG even though weighted is rollout-best, and cannot detect that both shuffled hard/soft branches are weak.
caveat: Stress diagnostic only; absolute success is modest; router-v2 abstention remains the honest final-method posture until a coverage-sensitive policy-quality proxy is validated

evidence: Positive-only NN top40 is the strongest non-oracle primary frozen Can 40p/80b endpoint row so far: 108/150 versus 99/150 for TRIAGE-BC, 90/150 for weighted BC, and 81/150 for all-demo BC. Fresh Can 20p/80b favors positive-only NN top20 over TRIAGE-BC on both completed endpoint splits: 54/100 versus 46/100 pooled, and its three-split support audit admits far fewer hidden-bad demos than TRIAGE: 11/240 versus 69/240. Fresh Can 80p/80b split 33 favors positive-only NN top80 by six successes: 49/50 versus 43/50 for TRIAGE-BC. Lift positive-only NN top160 is viable and close to bad-aware pos-min in the original 50-episode endpoint check: 0.560 versus 0.600; on three fresh frozen Lift splits it reaches 82/150 and exceeds TRIAGE-BC at 74/150
caveat: Treat no-bad-label retrieval as a first-class baseline; claim bad-label calibration only in regimes where it beats this control; diagnostic oracle is not deployable

claim: Full Tri-PIQL inverse-Q actor extraction beats all real-data baselines
evidence: Minari and early feed-forward/GMM real-data screens were negative or unstable
caveat: Current positive robotics evidence is selector or weighted sampling plus official BC-RNN-GMM
